steps.title: Identify coefficients from the standard form
Apply the quadratic formula
steps.content:
Apply the quadratic formula by substituting the coefficients:
  • Numerator: $-b = -5$
  • Denominator: $2a = 2(6) = 12$
  • Discriminant: $b^2 - 4ac = 5^2 - 4(6)(-7) = 25 - (-168) = 25 + 168$
Therefore, the solution is $x = \frac{-5 \pm \sqrt{25 + 168}}{12}$. That confirms A is correct.
